title: Flatten repo structure into templates/
body:
Move all installable files under a single `templates/` directory. Currently source files are split between `vendored/` (scripts) and `templates/` (config + workflows) for no clear reason.

## Acceptance Criteria

### File moves
- [ ] `vendored/add` → `templates/add`
- [ ] `vendored/update` → `templates/update`
- [ ] `vendored/check` → `templates/check`
- [ ] `vendored/hooks/pre-commit` → `templates/hooks/pre-commit`
- [ ] `templates/vendored/config.json` → `templates/config.json`
- [ ] `vendored/` directory deleted (empty after moves)
- [ ] `templates/vendored/` directory deleted (empty after moves)

### install.sh updates
- [ ] `fetch_file` calls reference `templates/add`, `templates/update`, `templates/check`, `templates/hooks/pre-commit` instead of `vendored/...`
- [ ] Config template fetched from `templates/config.json` instead of `templates/vendored/config.json`
- [ ] install.sh still passes all existing test_installer.py tests (after path updates in tests)

### Housekeeping
- [ ] `.semver/config.json` `files` array: remove `vendored/*` (now redundant since `templates/*` already covers it)
- [ ] All test imports updated: `ROOT / "vendored" / "add"` → `ROOT / "templates" / "add"`, etc.
- [ ] All existing tests pass with the new paths

### Out of scope
- Renaming scripts (add→install, etc.) — that's gv-cf3f.2
- Manifest storage — that's gv-cf3f.3

title: Merge add+update into unified install command
body:
Create a single `templates/install` script that replaces both `templates/add` and `templates/update`. The command is idempotent: it detects whether the vendor exists and handles both first-time add and update flows.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Unified command
- [ ] `templates/install` exists, executable, handles both add and update
- [ ] Usage: `.vendored/install <owner/repo> [--version <version>] [--force] [--pr] [--name <name>]`
- [ ] If vendor not registered: pre-validate (repo exists, has install.sh, version resolvable), run install.sh, post-validate (self-registered with required fields)
- [ ] If vendor already registered: resolve version, skip if already at target (unless `--force`), download and run install.sh
- [ ] `--pr` flag creates a PR with changes (same behavior as current `update --pr`)
- [ ] `--name` flag overrides vendor key name (same behavior as current `add --name`)
- [ ] Structured output preserved: vendor, old_version, new_version, changed (key=value lines, GITHUB_OUTPUT)

### Removed files
- [ ] `templates/add` deleted
- [ ] `templates/update` deleted

### Workflow update
- [ ] `templates/github/workflows/install-vendored.yml` calls `.vendored/install` instead of `.vendored/update`
- [ ] Workflow input semantics unchanged (vendor name, version)

### install.sh bootstrap update
- [ ] `install.sh` fetches `templates/install` → `.vendored/install` (instead of separate add + update)
- [ ] `install.sh` no longer fetches or installs `.vendored/add` or `.vendored/update`
- [ ] `install.sh` cleans up old `.vendored/add` and `.vendored/update` on upgrade

### Tests
- [ ] `tests/test_install.py` created, covering both add-path and update-path flows
- [ ] Tests cover: version skip logic, --force override, pre-validation (add path), structured output, PR creation, multi-vendor "all" mode
- [ ] `tests/test_add.py` and `tests/test_update.py` deleted
- [ ] `tests/test_installer.py` updated for new paths (.vendored/install instead of .vendored/add + .vendored/update)
- [ ] All tests pass

title: Add manifest emission to the vendor install contract
body:
Shift from "vendor self-registers in config" to "vendor emits a file manifest, framework handles the rest." The framework sets env vars before running install.sh, reads the manifest after, and stores it alongside a version file.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Environment variables passed to install.sh
- [ ] Framework sets `VENDOR_REPO` (owner/repo) before running install.sh
- [ ] Framework sets `VENDOR_REF` (git ref, e.g. `v1.2.0`) before running install.sh
- [ ] Framework sets `VENDOR_MANIFEST` (path to temp file for manifest output) before running install.sh
- [ ] Framework passes `GH_TOKEN` for authenticated API access
- [ ] Version is no longer passed as a positional arg to install.sh (`bash install.sh <version>` → just `bash install.sh`)

### Post-install manifest handling
- [ ] Framework reads `$VENDOR_MANIFEST` after install.sh completes
- [ ] Validation: manifest is non-empty (at least one file listed)
- [ ] Validation: every file listed in manifest exists on disk
- [ ] Validation failure exits non-zero with a clear error message
- [ ] Manifest stored at `.vendored/manifests/<vendor>.files` (one path per line, no trailing newline noise)
- [ ] Version stored at `.vendored/manifests/<vendor>.version` (single line)
- [ ] `.vendored/manifests/` directory created automatically if missing

### Config.json update
- [ ] `protected` field derived from manifest contents (all files in manifest + the manifest file itself + the version file)
- [ ] Existing user-specified `allowed` entries preserved
- [ ] `install_branch` and `repo` fields still set/updated as before

### Backwards compatibility (v1 vendors)
- [ ] If install.sh does not write to `$VENDOR_MANIFEST` (v1 vendor), framework falls back to detecting config changes (current behavior)
- [ ] If install.sh self-registers in config (v1 behavior), framework extracts the info and does not error
- [ ] On next install of a v1 vendor that starts emitting manifests, the transition is seamless

### Tests
- [ ] Tests for env var setup: VENDOR_REPO, VENDOR_REF, VENDOR_MANIFEST passed correctly
- [ ] Tests for manifest validation: empty manifest, missing files, valid manifest
- [ ] Tests for manifest storage: correct paths, correct content
- [ ] Tests for v1 fallback: vendor that doesn't write manifest still works
- [ ] Tests for `protected` derivation from manifest
- [ ] All existing tests still pass

title: Derive protection rules from manifests in check
body:
Update the check command to read protection rules from `.vendored/manifests/<vendor>.files` instead of the `protected` field in config.json. This completes the shift to manifest-driven protection.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Manifest-based protection
- [ ] `check` reads `.vendored/manifests/<vendor>.files` for each registered vendor
- [ ] Protected set = files listed in manifest + `.vendored/manifests/<vendor>.files` + `.vendored/manifests/<vendor>.version`
- [ ] Changed files matched against the manifest-derived protected set (exact path match, not glob)
- [ ] `allowed` exceptions from config.json still apply

### Backwards compatibility
- [ ] If `.vendored/manifests/<vendor>.files` does not exist, fall back to `protected` globs from config.json (v1 behavior)
- [ ] Fallback uses existing glob matching logic (fnmatch with ** support)
- [ ] Mixed state works: some vendors have manifests, others use config `protected`

### Config migration
- [ ] When `install` runs and creates a manifest, the `protected` field is removed from that vendor's config entry
- [ ] This is additive to gv-cf3f.3 — the install command should stop writing `protected` to config once manifests are in use

### Branch bypass
- [ ] Install branch bypass logic unchanged: if branch matches `install_branch` prefix, skip that vendor's checks

### Tests
- [ ] Tests for manifest-based protection: exact path matching from manifest files
- [ ] Tests for fallback to config `protected` when no manifest exists
- [ ] Tests for mixed state: one vendor with manifest, another without
- [ ] Tests for manifest files themselves being protected (the .files and .version files)
- [ ] Tests for `allowed` exceptions still working with manifest-based protection
- [ ] All existing check tests still pass (with any necessary fixture updates)

title: Create remove command for clean vendor uninstall
body:
Add a new `templates/remove` command that uses the manifest to cleanly uninstall a vendor — deleting all its files, its manifest, and its config entry.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Command interface
- [ ] `templates/remove` exists, executable Python 3 script
- [ ] Usage: `.vendored/remove <vendor>`
- [ ] Exits non-zero with clear error if vendor not found in config.json
- [ ] Exits non-zero with clear error if no manifest exists for the vendor (and no `protected` fallback for delete — too risky without a manifest)

### Removal flow
- [ ] Reads `.vendored/manifests/<vendor>.files` to get list of files to delete
- [ ] Deletes every file listed in the manifest
- [ ] Deletes `.vendored/manifests/<vendor>.files`
- [ ] Deletes `.vendored/manifests/<vendor>.version`
- [ ] Removes the vendor entry from `.vendored/config.json`
- [ ] Cleans up empty parent directories left behind (e.g., if all files in `.some-tool/` are deleted, remove `.some-tool/`)

### Output
- [ ] Reports each file deleted
- [ ] Reports summary: "Removed vendor: <name> (N files deleted)"
- [ ] Handles already-missing files gracefully (warn, don't error — file may have been manually deleted)

### Bootstrap
- [ ] `install.sh` fetches `templates/remove` → `.vendored/remove` and makes it executable
- [ ] `.vendored/remove` included in git-vendored's own self-registered protected files

### Tests
- [ ] Test: successful removal deletes all manifest files, manifest itself, and config entry
- [ ] Test: error when vendor not in config
- [ ] Test: error when no manifest file exists
- [ ] Test: graceful handling of already-missing files in manifest
- [ ] Test: empty directory cleanup after file deletion
- [ ] Test: config.json still valid JSON after removal (other vendors unaffected)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass

title: Rewrite bootstrap install.sh for v2 manifest contract
body:
Rewrite git-vendored's own `install.sh` (the bootstrap) to conform to the v2 vendor contract: read env vars instead of positional args, emit a manifest instead of self-registering in config, and source files from the flattened `templates/` layout.

## Acceptance Criteria

### v2 contract compliance
- [ ] install.sh reads `VENDOR_REF` instead of `$1` for version (fall back to `$1` for backwards compat during transition)
- [ ] install.sh reads `VENDOR_REPO` instead of `$2` for repo (fall back to `$2` / hardcoded default)
- [ ] install.sh writes a manifest to `$VENDOR_MANIFEST` listing every file it creates/modifies
- [ ] install.sh does NOT self-register in `.vendored/config.json` (framework does this now)
- [ ] install.sh does NOT write `.vendored/.version` (framework writes `.vendored/manifests/git-vendored.version`)

### Manifest contents
The manifest must list every file installed:
- [ ] `.vendored/install`
- [ ] `.vendored/check`
- [ ] `.vendored/remove`
- [ ] `.vendored/hooks/pre-commit`
- [ ] `.github/workflows/install-vendored.yml`
- [ ] `.github/workflows/check-vendor.yml`

### Directory setup
- [ ] Creates `.vendored/`, `.vendored/hooks/`, `.vendored/manifests/`, `.github/workflows/` directories
- [ ] `.vendored/config.json` only created if missing (preserve existing — same as current behavior)

### Source paths
- [ ] All `fetch_file` calls reference `templates/` paths (already done in gv-cf3f.1, verify still correct)

### Cleanup
- [ ] Removes deprecated `.vendored/add`, `.vendored/update` (migrating from v1)
- [ ] Removes deprecated `.vendored/.version` (replaced by manifests/<vendor>.version)

### Tests
- [ ] test_installer.py updated for v2 contract: env var inputs, manifest output, no config self-registration
- [ ] Test: manifest file written to $VENDOR_MANIFEST path with correct contents
- [ ] Test: config.json not modified by install.sh (framework's job)
- [ ] Test: backwards compat — positional args still work when env vars not set
- [ ] Test: deprecated files cleaned up on upgrade
- [ ] All tests pass

### README
- [ ] Update README "Vendor Contract" section to document the v2 env var contract
- [ ] Update README "Quick Start" section if bootstrap invocation changes
- [ ] Update config schema docs: `protected` no longer in config, derived from manifests
- [ ] Add migration note for v1 → v2

title: Remove command should error without manifest instead of falling back to pattern scan
install.sh doesn't read VENDOR_REF/VENDOR_REPO env vars per v2 contract
install.sh still self-registers in config.json (v2 contract says framework handles this)
install.sh still writes deprecated .vendored/.version (should use manifests only)
README not updated for v2 manifest contract (vendor contract, quick start, config schema, migration note)
